In __set_name__, store the private attribute name: self.private_name = f'_{name}'. This runs automatically when the class body finishes, so 'volume' becomes '_volume'.
In __set__, clamp the value: clamped = max(self.min_val, min(self.max_val, value)). Then store it on the instance: setattr(instance, self.private_name, clamped).
In __get__, use getattr(instance, self.private_name, self.min_val). The third argument to getattr is the default — this handles the case where no value has been set yet.
The descriptor must store values on each instance separately (via setattr on the instance). Never store the value on self (the descriptor) — that would make all instances share one value.
The __init__ in AudioSettings just does self.volume = volume and self.bass = bass — those assignments automatically go through the descriptors' __set__ methods.
